data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/20/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Plunge amid Trump's Greenland Tariff Threat
data.description:
U.S. stock futures dropped early Tuesday, starting the holiday-shortened trading week on a negative note. President Donald Trump has threatened a new round of tariffs over his demand to acquire Greenland, rattling global markets. U.S. markets were closed on Monday in honor of the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.76%, 1.51%, and 1.38%, respectively, at 3:51 a.m. EST on January 20.

Last week, all three major averages closed lower, despite the rally in semiconductor stocks on Thursday after Taiwan Semiconductor's (TSM) blockbuster results. The S&P 500 declined 0.4%, the DJIA fell 0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.7%.

In a Saturday post on Truth Social, Trump announced a 10% U.S. tariff on eight European allies, including the UK, effective February 1. The tariffs will eventually rise to 25% on June 1 and will stay in place until the U.S. strikes a deal to acquire Greenland.

Looking forward, investors will focus on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos on Wednesday, where he will discuss the Greenland issue.

The earnings season also remains in focus. Notable companies reporting this week include Netflix (NFLX), United Airlines (UAL), 3M (MMM), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Charles Schwab (SCHW), Intel (INTC), and Visa (V).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.15 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,721 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today amid Trump's renewed tariff threats. European countries are reportedly discussing counter-tariffs and wider economic penalties in response.

Moreover, this morning, Trump threatened 200% tariffs on French wine and champagne after reports stated that France's President Macron refused to join his "Board of Peace" on Gaza.

Asia-Pacific Markets Fall

Asia-Pacific markets fell on January 20 due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, including those involving Iran and Greenland.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 0.29%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slipped 0.01%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.52%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei dropped 1.11%, and the Topix declined 0.84%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'AI War Heats Up': Alibaba Stock (BABA) Slips as ByteDance Ramps Up AI Cloud Push
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) shares were trending lower in pre-market trading as investors reacted to signs of rising competition in China's fast-growing AI cloud market. The pressure comes after ByteDance, the owner of TikTok, stepped up efforts to expand its AI-led cloud business.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ByteDance has been growing its cloud unit, Volcano Engine, and is using its AI tools to win corporate customers. The company is also offering lower prices to attract new clients. This strategy has made ByteDance more competitive in AI-heavy workloads, where demand from businesses is rising quickly.

These moves are starting to challenge Alibaba's lead in the fast-growing AI cloud space.

Market Share Data Shows ByteDance Closing the Gap

That push is now starting to show up in market share numbers. According to research firm IDC, Alibaba remained China's largest AI cloud provider in the first half of 2025, with about 23% of the market. ByteDance ranked second with close to 13%, making it the fastest-rising challenger in the segment.

While ByteDance's share of China's overall cloud market is still relatively small, analysts believe its sharp focus on AI services is helping it grow much faster than many rivals.

What It Means for Alibaba Investors

Alibaba remains the clear leader in China's AI cloud market, but ByteDance's rapid rise shows that competition is heating up.

As AI demand continues to grow, Alibaba investors will be watching closely to see how well the company defends its position and whether rising rivalry begins to weigh on margins and growth.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now?  

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 13 Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.66, suggesting roughly 23.13% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: After U.S. Approval, Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI Chip Waits on China's Answer
data.description:
NVIDIA (NVDA) received approval from the U.S. last week to sell its H200 AI chip to China under set terms. However, the process has now slowed because China has not cleared the chip for entry. According to reports, Chinese customs have told agents that the H200 is not allowed into the country for now. As a result, shipments remain on hold even though the U.S. side has agreed.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This creates an unusual setup for investors to track. In most past cases, U.S. export rules were the main limit on chip sales to China. In this case, the delay appears to be coming from Beijing instead.

On Friday, NVDA shares dropped 0.44% to close at $186.23.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN: Trump's Greenland Gambit Rattles Big Tech as EU Weighs Salvo
data.description:
President Donald Trump's tariff threat tied to his Greenland acquisition ambitions, along with the EU's mooted $108 billion tariff package and "trade bazooka," sank tech shares in U.S. pre-market trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As trading resumed on Tuesday following Monday's federal holidays, Big Tech stocks fell, mimicking the fall in U.S. stock futures and a similar drop in Europe seen earlier on Monday. As of 4:42 a.m. EST:

  • Nvidia (NVDA) — dropped 3.17% to $180.27
  • Alphabet (GOOGL) — tumbled 4% to $316.86
  • Amazon (AMZN) — shrank 3.20% to $218.48
  • Microsoft (MSFT) — edged lower 1.71% to $451.98
  • Meta (META) — fell about 3% to $601.71

Over the weekend, President Trump warned of an additional 10% tariff on eight European countries, starting February 1, with plans to raise them to 25% by June 1 until a deal is reached on his proposal to make Greenland part of the U.S. In addition to the EU countries of Denmark, Sweden, France, Germany and the Netherland, the threat covers non-members, the UK and Norway.

What EU and U.S. Stocks Could Be in Crosshairs?

Trump's new tariffs could leave several European companies, including Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO), Switzerland-based Roche (RHHBY), and France's Sanofi (SNY), exposed to additional export burden. In response, the EU is considering letting its suspended retaliatory tariff package on $108 billion American goods come into effect in February.

Such a move could hit several U.S. companies such as Tyson Foods (TSN), Harley-Davidson (HOG) and Caterpillar (CAT).

In addition, the EU is weighing invoking its anti-coercion instrument, colloquially known as the "trade bazooka." However, EU leaders will meet this Thursday in Brussels for an emergency summit to discuss their options.

data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: NFLX vs. DIS: Which Stock Is the Better Buy Before Earnings?
data.description:
Streaming giants Netflix (NFLX) and Disney (DIS) are set to report their quarterly earnings, and investors are weighing which stock offers the better opportunity. Netflix will release Q4 2025 results on January 20, while Disney is scheduled to report Q1 FY26 on February 2. Ahead of earnings, Disney stock carries a Strong Buy rating from analysts, with upside potential of over 20%. In comparison, Netflix has a Moderate Buy rating but offers a higher potential upside of more than 40%.

data.title: OpenAI Reports $20 Billion Revenue Run Rate as AI Computing Demand Accelerates
data.description:
OpenAI shared new details showing how rapidly demand for AI tools is growing. The company said its annual revenue pace passed $20 billion in 2025, up from $6 billion in 2024. At the same time, OpenAI said its computing capacity rose to about 1.9 gigawatts from 0.6 gigawatts a year earlier. Together, these numbers show that growth in AI use is moving in step with higher spending on computing power.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, OpenAI said its weekly and daily user activity continues to reach record levels. This suggests that AI tools are becoming part of daily work and consumer habits.

What This Means for Public Market Investors

The growing use of AI tools also supports other companies across the industry. OpenAI is closely tied to Microsoft (MSFT), which provides key cloud services for its AI systems. As OpenAI expands its computing needs, Microsoft benefits from higher cloud usage and deeper ties to major AI products. At the same time, the growth in computing demand supports suppliers such as Nvidia (NVDA), which provides chips used in AI data centers.

Meanwhile, OpenAI announced it will begin showing ads in ChatGPT to some U.S. users. This move points to a focus on steady revenue to help cover high operating costs. In simple terms, ads help support continued growth without relying only on enterprise deals or new funding.

Looking ahead, OpenAI said it plans to focus on AI agents and tools that can run tasks over time across different systems. The company also noted it plans to keep a light balance sheet by partnering rather than owning major hardware assets. Due to this approach, much of the financial upside from AI growth continues to flow to public companies that provide cloud services, chips, and infrastructure.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Gemini. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Is Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Stock a Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) is scheduled to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on Wednesday, January 21. Analysts expect a sharp rise in earnings but a modest growth in sales, as the world's largest healthcare products maker pursues portfolio diversification. Investors will pay particular attention to JNJ's FY26 guidance to assess the demand across both its drug portfolio and medical devices segment.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

JNJ shares have surged more than 47% over the past year, outpacing the S&P 500's (SPX) return of 14.7%. Johnson & Johnson is known as the Dividend King, with over 60 years of consistent dividend increases. JNJ currently pays a quarterly dividend of $1.30 per share, reflecting an above-industry yield of 2.50%, and a payout ratio of 49.52%. The company also conducts regular share buybacks, rewarding shareholders with a disciplined cash flow allocation approach.

Expectations from JNJ

The Street expects JNJ to report a 22% jump in earnings per share (EPS) to $2.49 in Q4. Meanwhile, sales are forecast to grow 7.3% year-over-year to $24.16 billion. The company boasts a robust pipeline of both commercialized and under-testing drugs, with a focus on oncology, immunology, neuroscience, cardiovascular, surgery, and vision.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: M&A News: GSK Bets Big on Food Allergy Drugs with $2.2B RAPT Therapeutics Deal
data.description:
GlaxoSmithKline (GSK) has agreed to acquire RAPT Therapeutics (RAPT) for $2.2 billion to strengthen its pipeline of treatments for food allergies and other inflammatory diseases. The deal gives GSK access to a promising experimental drug, ozureprubart, that could change how severe food allergies are treated.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Under the agreed terms, GSK will pay $58 per share in cash, valuing RAPT at $2.2 billion. Net of cash acquired, GSK's estimated upfront investment is $1.9 billion. The deal is expected to close in Q1 2026, pending regulatory approval.

It is worth noting that GSK will gain global rights to ozureprubart, except in mainland China, Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan. Also, it will assume milestone and royalty obligations tied to RAPT's partnership with Shanghai Jeyou Pharmaceutical.

Here's Why the Deal Matters to GSK

At the core of the deal is ozureprubart, RAPT's long‑acting antibody drug that helps protect patients from severe allergic reactions. The drug targets IgE, the key driver behind about 94% of severe food allergies.

Current anti‑IgE treatments require injections every 2 to 4 weeks, which is tough for children, who make up a large share of food allergy patients. Ozureprubart may cut the dosage down to once every 12 weeks, making treatment much easier to manage. GSK said the drug could also help 25% of patients who are not eligible for existing therapies.

Importantly, phase IIb data is expected in 2027, with Phase III trials planned for both adults and children at high risk of severe reactions.

Overall, the deal strengthens GSK's focus on specialty medicines and boosts its position in immunology. With ozureprubart, GSK is betting on a therapy that could become best-in-class if the data proves strong.

Is GSK a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Sell consensus rating on GSK stock based on one Buy, two Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months. Further, the average GSK price target of $38.69 per share implies 19.76% downside risk.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: AstraZeneca (AZN) to Move U.S. Listing from Nasdaq to NYSE
data.description:
AstraZeneca (AZN) said on Tuesday that it will delist its American Depositary Shares and debt securities from Nasdaq and move its U.S. listing to the New York Stock Exchange.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said the delisting from Nasdaq will take effect after the market closes on January 30, 2026. Trading of AstraZeneca's ordinary shares and debt securities on the NYSE is expected to begin on February 2, under the same AZN ticker.

The switch is part of a plan approved by shareholders to simplify how the company's shares trade around the world. Under the new setup, investors will be able to trade the company's ordinary shares directly across three major markets: the London Stock Exchange, Nasdaq Stockholm, and the NYSE.

What Changes for U.S. Investors

AstraZeneca will move away from American Depositary Shares, which currently represent two ordinary shares. Instead, U.S. investors will trade the company's $0.25 ordinary shares directly on the NYSE.

By switching to a direct listing, AstraZeneca aims to simplify trading for global investors and reduce complexity across markets. The company said the unified structure should make its shares easier to understand and trade, while keeping access open to investors in the U.S., the U.K., and Europe.

Is AZN a Good Stock to Buy Now? 

On TipRanks, AZN has a Strong Buy consensus based on 5 Buy ratings. Its highest price target is $108. AZN stock's consensus price target is $102.75, implying a 8.86% upside.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Elliott Backs Private Hypersonic Firm as U.S. Defense Spending Accelerates
data.description:
The Wall Street Journal reported that Elliott Investment Management has made a large investment in Stratolaunch, a private defense company focused on hypersonic flight. The report said the stake is worth several hundred million dollars and includes a board seat.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stratolaunch is based in California and is owned by Cerberus Capital Management. Cerberus bought the company in 2019 after the death of founder Paul Allen. Since then, the company has shifted away from space launch work and toward defense testing.

Why This Investment Matters

Elliott is best known as an activist hedge fund, so its move into private defense technology stands out. The firm usually looks for clear paths to growth and tighter control over spending. As a result, its involvement suggests Stratolaunch is moving closer to stable defense revenue.

Meanwhile, the company is focused on reusable aircraft that test hypersonic systems. These vehicles fly faster than five times the speed of sound and are used to reduce test costs for the U.S. military. In March, the Pentagon completed successful test flights using Stratolaunch's Talon A vehicle.

The Broader Defense Backdrop

More capital is flowing into hypersonic technology across the U.S. PitchBook estimates that more than $2 billion went into this space last year. However, past efforts by the U.S. have moved slowly compared with China and Russia.

Against this backdrop, defense officials have called hypersonics a top priority. As a result, companies that offer testing and support tools may benefit first. Stratolaunch fits that role by helping the military test weapons faster and at lower cost.

In simple terms, Elliott's investment signals rising confidence that hypersonic testing will become a core part of future defense spending.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to line up public defense and aerospace companies with exposure to hypersonics, advanced weapons, or defense testing infrastructure. It's a great tool to gain an in-depth view of each stock and the broader aerospace and defense industry.
data.author: Ran Melamed
